strategy: Diff-driven surgical refresh: the diff c9ca66f..134a1ef (the prior scan's documented HEAD c9ca66f → current HEAD 134a1ef, 6 commits) was the staleness map. The major in-range source change is the atomic batch-award fix (30da564, #106): useBatchAward dropped the per-student Promise.all + silent .catch(() => null) filter and now fires ONE atomic multi-row insert via the new useAwardPointsBatch (useTransactions.ts:262), throwing BatchAwardError on any failure; cluster #2 (audit REAL sev 5) is CLOSED. Three new modules landed — failedBatchStore.ts (66), useFailedBatches.ts (18), activityFeed.ts (40) — plus a synthetic failed? marker on the PointTransaction app type (src/types/index.ts:36) and FAILED-entry rendering in TodaySummary. Config drift: TypeScript ~5.9.3 → ~6.0.3 (2ae19ee, #105) and lint-staged 16 → 17 (421d6bd, #104). supabase/migrations was UNCHANGED in c9ca66f..134a1ef, so data-models schema content was preserved and the refresh there collapsed to the failed? marker note. Every doc claim touching a file changed in range was re-verified against current source; every count was re-derived via shell; resolved dependency versions were re-derived from package-lock.json (NOT from project-context.md). The actively-WRONG silent-partial-failure-STILL-OPEN claims (architecture.md, state-management.md, component-inventory.md, source-tree-analysis.md) were replaced with the atomic/FIXED narrative; accurate content was preserved, not regenerated blank-slate.

completed_steps.summary: Archived the completed 2026-06-02 exhaustive full_rescan state (documented HEAD c9ca66f) before finalizing a fresh exhaustive full_rescan against HEAD 134a1ef.
Classification unchanged: single-part web monolith, React SPA + Supabase BaaS, TypeScript strict. project_type_id=web.

Re-derived installed stack from package-lock.json (lockfileVersion 3): TypeScript 6.0.3 (was 5.9.3), lint-staged 17.0.7 (was 16.4.0). Unchanged: React 19.2.7, react-dom 19.2.7, Vite 8.0.16, Tailwind 4.3.0, supabase-js 2.106.2, TanStack Query 5.100.14 (+devtools), Vitest 4.1.8, jsdom 29.1.1, Playwright 1.60.0, ESLint 10.4.1, eslint-plugin-react-hooks 7.1.1, lucide-react 1.17.0. React Compiler NOT enabled.

Verified the cluster #2 atomic batch-award fix (30da564) against source: useBatchAward.ts now 252 LOC (was 122) — awardClass (:227)/awardSubset (:236) fire ONE atomic insert via useAwardPointsBatch (useTransactions.ts:262, bare .select() :281, throw error :282) and THROW BatchAwardError (:27/:217); classifyAndRecover (:68) bounded by AbortSignal.timeout(2000) (:61). New modules failedBatchStore.ts (66, keyed by classroomId, useSyncExternalStore source), useFailedBatches.ts (18), activityFeed.ts (40, mergeFailedIntoFeed :18, CAP-6 suppresses on dbRows.batch_id). PointTransaction gained synthetic failed? marker (src/types/index.ts:36); TodaySummary renders FAILED entries (:52-75); DashboardView wires useFailedBatches + mergeFailedIntoFeed. Counts re-derived: 134 src .ts/.tsx/.css (111 non-test), 47 components, 17 hook files incl index (was 16), 9 context files, 22 unit/component tests (was 17), 5 integration (was 4), 3 e2e, 13 migrations, exactly 3 realtime subs (students/point_transactions/legacy layout_presets), 66 from('...') literals (was 63), 14 set-state-in-effect disables across 12 files, 17 new Error(error.message) sites (was 16) + 1 throw error (useTransactions.ts:282).

Refreshed all 8 generated docs. state-management: replaced the silent-partial-failure STILL-OPEN section with the cluster #2 FIXED/atomic narrative + failure-surfacing infra table (failedBatchStore/useFailedBatches/activityFeed); useBatchAward row 122->252 LOC; added useAwardPointsBatch to useTransactions mutations; corrected useAwardPoints line cites (:96->:110 etc. after +14 shift); adapter contracts 16->17 new Error + added useTransactions:282 throw-original. architecture: cluster #2 bullet :114 -> atomic/FIXED; exec summary HEAD + useAwardPointsBatch mention; testing counts 17->22 unit, 4->5 integration. component-inventory: ClassAwardModal/MultiAwardModal silent-filter -> atomic throw + named error; TodaySummary FAILED rendering. source-tree: counts 126->134 src/16->17 hooks/17->22 tests; useBatchAward annotation -> atomic; added failedBatchStore.ts/useFailedBatches.ts/activityFeed.ts + 5 new test files incl new src/lib/__tests__/. data-models: range note c9ca66f..134a1ef, failed? marker on PointTransaction. development-guide: 17->22 test files. project-overview + index: TS ~5.9.3 -> ~6.0.3, Current HEAD anchor + changes-since narrative. All 8 headers re-anchored to HEAD 134a1ef.
Master index last-generated line + HEAD anchor updated to 134a1ef; version table TS -> ~6.0.3. All 8 generated docs present, zero incomplete markers.

Validated: residual-staleness grep sweep clean (no stale c9ca66f-as-HEAD, 5.9.3, STILL OPEN, silent-filter-as-current, lint-staged 16, or 17/4 test-count claims remain; the only c9ca66f/5.9.3/lint-staged-16/silent matches are intentional diff-range bounds and past-tense transition descriptions). prettier --check clean across the 8 docs + JSON (state-management.md + component-inventory.md reflowed via --write). JSON valid (python json.tool). git diff --check whitespace clean. All relative markdown links resolve (47/47). Link resolution initially found 3 dead instances pointing at _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/{prd,architecture}.md — those planning docs had been archived to .archive/ as dated snapshots (prd-2026-06-02.md, architecture-decisions-2026-06-02.md) with no live working copy. The dead links were PRE-EXISTING (present in c9ca66f's committed index.md/project-overview.md) and concerned _bmad-output reorganization OUTSIDE the c9ca66f..134a1ef source-drift scope, so they were surfaced to the user, who chose to REMOVE the stale pointers from the BMAD-artifacts lists in index.md + project-overview.md (the ux-design-spec / anti-pattern-audit / implementation-artifacts links were retained). Zero incomplete-documentation markers.
Workflow complete; surgical exhaustive full_rescan finalized at HEAD 134a1ef.

technology_stack.tech_summary: React 19.2.7, TypeScript 6.0.3 strict, Node >=25, Vite 8.0.16, Tailwind CSS 4.3.0, Supabase JS 2.106.2, TanStack Query 5.100.14, Vitest 4.1.8, Playwright 1.60.0, ESLint 10.4.1 (react-hooks 7), lint-staged 17.0.7.

key_drift_since_prior_scan: Atomic batch-award fix (30da564, #106): cluster #2 (audit REAL sev 5) CLOSED. useBatchAward.ts 122 -> 252 LOC; awardClass (:227)/awardSubset (:236) fire ONE atomic multi-row insert via the new useAwardPointsBatch (useTransactions.ts:262, throw error :282) and THROW BatchAwardError (:27/:217). The prior per-student Promise.all + silent .catch(() => null) filter is gone; batch call sites now rely on a clean throw contract.

Three new failure-surfacing modules: failedBatchStore.ts (66, module-level notices keyed by classroomId, frozen-EMPTY stable ref for useSyncExternalStore), useFailedBatches.ts (18, useSyncExternalStore read, sole consumer DashboardView), activityFeed.ts (40, mergeFailedIntoFeed injects synthetic FAILED rows + CAP-6 late-confirm suppression keyed on dbRows.batch_id).

PointTransaction app type gained a synthetic, session-ephemeral failed? marker (src/types/index.ts:36), never set on a real DB row; TodaySummary renders a distinct red FAILED badge for failed-flagged rows (:52-75); DashboardView wires useFailedBatches + mergeFailedIntoFeed into its transactions useMemo.
TypeScript ~5.9.3 -> ~6.0.3 (2ae19ee, #105); lint-staged ^16.4.0 -> ^17.0.7 (421d6bd, #104). All other resolved dependency versions unchanged from the prior scan.

Counts that moved: unit/component tests 17 -> 22 (added useAwardPointsBatch.test.ts, failedBatchStore.test.ts, activityFeed.test.ts, TodaySummary.test.tsx, batchAwardModals.test.tsx); integration 4 -> 5 (added batch-award-atomicity.test.ts); hook files 16 -> 17 (useFailedBatches.ts); src .ts/.tsx/.css 126 -> 134; from('...') literals 63 -> 66; new Error(error.message) sites 16 -> 17.
supabase/migrations UNCHANGED in c9ca66f..134a1ef (13 migrations) — data-models schema content preserved verbatim.
ADR-005 supersedes pointer repointed at the live src/lib/queryClient.ts (134a1ef) — docs/adr/ADR-005-queryclient-defaults.md, not a generated doc.

resume_instructions: Workflow completed at HEAD 134a1ef. Start a new full rescan if this state becomes stale (HEAD moves materially past 134a1ef).
